sentence_reviews.text: Data augmentation and synthetic-data generation expand training variation through transformations, simulation, model generation, or controlled perturbation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Deep Learning, Chapter 7.4 dataset augmentation and Chapters 20 on generative models; GAN, VAE, diffusion, and flow-matching papers for model-based generation.
sentence_reviews.notes: Augmentation via transformations and synthetic/model-generated data are supported across the cited sources, though simulation and controlled perturbation are generalized.

sentence_reviews.text: Results are sensitive to data quality, hyperparameters, compute budget, random variation, and evaluation contamination. Its direct dependencies include Datasets, sampling & splits, Multimodal, simulated & streaming data.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Synthesized from cited node sources plus the atlas dependency context in data/atlas.json.
sentence_reviews.notes: Limitation is reviewed as a broad synthesis/tradeoff statement for the family-level node, not as a direct quotation from one source.

sentence_reviews.text: In this atlas graph, Data augmentation & synthetic-data generation is modeled as depending on Datasets, sampling & splits, Multimodal, simulated & streaming data and feeding downstream nodes such as Robustness, distribution shift & OOD evaluation, Reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR), Domain randomization & simulation curricula.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: data/atlas.json canonical node and dependency graph; generated from node dependencies and downstream consumers.
sentence_reviews.notes: This is an explicit internal atlas-graph positioning claim, not an external-world necessity claim.
